en: What's OpenClaw?
I've been programming since I was 14.
Building software felt like playing a video game.
zh: 开发软件对我来说就像在打游戏一样，

en: I couldn't stop.
Until I did.
I created a company,
I poured a decade of my life into it.
No venture capital,
every weekend.
And then I sold the dream.
zh: 后来我把这个梦想变现了，

en: And I felt absolutely nothing.
For three years,
I was wondering,
did therapy, traveled,
zh: 接受心理治疗、到处旅行，

en: I changed countries twice.
zh: 甚至换了两个国家生活。

en: Nothing, nothing clicked.
zh: 没有任何事能让我内心起波澜了。

en: Now I wake up every morning
with everything I was supposed to want
zh: 拥有我本该想要的一切，

en: and no reason.
No reason to get out of bed.
And then in early 2025,
I tried an experiment.
I wanted to see what these new AI coding agents are all about.
zh: 看看这些新出的AI编程智能体 到底是什么。

en: And I had what I can only describe as a "holy shit" moment.
zh: 然后我就经历了一次 只能用“我靠”来形容的时刻。

en: The boilerplate, the plumbing,
zh: 样板代码、底层连接逻辑，

en: all the boring parts that was software development,
zh: 软件开发里所有枯燥乏味的部分，

en: AI could do all of it.
The bottleneck is no longer typing.
zh: 软件开发的瓶颈不再是代码怎么写，

en: It's thinking.
And thinking was the part I did [for] 25 years.
zh: 思考正是我25年来一直做的事。

en: Building software felt like playing a video game again.
zh: 做软件又变得像打游戏一样了。

en: I was back.
I built 44 projects in just a few months.
zh: 短短几个月，我做了44个项目。

en: And the latest one was a WhatsApp bot.
zh: 最近做的一个是 基于 WhatsApp 的聊天机器人。

en: I put it on my computer,
it talks through the apps that you already know.
And then I took it on a trip to Marrakesh.
zh: 后来我带着它去马拉喀什旅行，

en: You know, just to navigate around, to find restaurants, do translations.
zh: 就是用来导航、找餐厅、翻译之类的。

en: And at first, it didn't really feel right.
It felt too much like a tool,
not like a friend.
You know, too many bullet points, too many tables.
zh: 回复格式都是小圆点和表格。

en: So I told it,
because those modern models, they are so smart,
zh: 现在的模型真的很聪明，

en: they know what WhatsApp is, they know how people talk.
I just had to tell it.
zh: 我只需要告诉它怎么做。

en: And then it felt right.
And you know how you talk to friends --
at some point I was walking around and I was sending it a voice message.
zh: 有一次我边走边给它发了一条语音。

en: And then I froze because I actually,

zh: 因为我根本没做语音功能。

en: I added support for images, yeah, but even that took hours.
zh: 但图片支持我是加了， 光这个就用了好几个小时。

en: So I was looking at the typing indicator,
zh: 于是我盯着“对方正在输入...”，

en: and then the agent responded.
And I very vividly remember this situation
zh: 那一幕我记得清清楚楚——

en: where I was standing there and I was like,
how did you do that?
And the agent replied, I'm not kidding you,
zh: 它回我一句， 我没忽悠你们哈，

en: "The mad lad figured it out on its own."
(Laughter)
And then it walked me through every single step:
zh: 然后它一步一步告诉我：

en: how it got a message from me
zh: 收到了我发的一条消息，

en: but there was no file ending, so it inspected the file,
zh: 但没有文件扩展名， 于是对文件进行了检测，

en: it found that it was audio, but a weird format, so it converted it.
zh: 发现是一条音频， 但格式很奇怪，于是它做了格式转换。

en: And then it was looking for something to translate the audio,
zh: 随后它去找语音转文字的工具，

en: but I didn't have it installed.
But then it found an OpenAI key, it sent the whole thing to the server,
zh: 它找到一个OpenAI密钥， 把这些传到了服务器，

en: it got it back, and then it replied.
All of that in nine seconds.
Can you imagine? I didn't build any of that.
zh: 你敢信？这些我一行都没写过。

en: For me, this was the moment where I thought,
zh: 对我来说，就是在那一刻，我意识到：

en: this is something new.
This is not a chatbot.
Chatbots give up.
zh: 机器人遇到困难会放弃，

en: Agents improvise.
And, you know, I was sold.
I wanted to share this.
I wanted to like, tell people on Twitter.
And nobody really got it.
It's like, it's almost like you have to experience an agent.
zh: 就像是，智能体这种东西只能意会，

en: It's kind of hard to explain.
It took me a few weeks.
And then I did something stupid.
And remember, this agent, by default,
can do anything that you can do on your computer.
zh: 能做任何你能在电脑上做的事。

en: So obviously I put it in a public Discord, and I invited random people.
zh: 我把它扔进了一个公开Discord群里， 拉了一堆陌生人进来。

en: (Laughter)
And I was looking at it the whole night.
People were talking with it,
people were having fun with it, people tried to hack it.
zh: 玩得很开心，也有人试图攻击它。

en: And when my eyes were like, falling off almost,
zh: 后来眼皮实在撑不住了，

en: I exited the process, and I went to bed.
Though I forgot -- I built a system to be resilient.
zh: 但我忘了， 这个系统能自动重启的。

en: So while I was walking to the bedroom,
zh: 所以我走向卧室的时候，

en: the agent happily booted up again and talked to everyone in the world.
zh: 智能体已经自己重新启动， 开开心心地跟全世界的人聊上了。

en: The next morning I woke up --
over 800 messages.
I panicked.
I pulled the plug,
I read every single message
just to see if the agent leaked my private life.
zh: 生怕它把我的私生活全抖出去了。

en: (Laughter)
Nothing happened.
But it could have.
But that was the moment where it went viral.
zh: 也就是在那一刻，它火了。

en: Today, the project is called OpenClaw.
It's pretty much the fastest-growing open-source project.
zh: 基本上是增长最快的开源项目。

en: Its mascot is a lobster.
It claws into your machine.
zh: 它会把爪子伸进你的机器。

en: Jensen Huang calls it the operating system for personal AI.
zh: 黄仁勋称它为个人 AI 操作系统。

en: But by far, my favorite quote is from a friend
zh: 但目前我最喜欢一位朋友的评价，

en: who looked at this statistic and said,
zh: 他看了这张统计图后说：

en: "Peter, this is not hockey-stick growth.
This is stripper pole."
(Laughter)
And I, I was not ready.
You know, when something blows up like this,
zh: 众所周知，一旦某件事出圈了，

en: everything explodes.
Hundreds of messages,
reporters calling me in the middle of the night,
security vulnerabilities.
zh: 还有各种安全漏洞要处理。

en: And then
the AI company whose model most of my users loved
zh: 我大部分用户最喜欢用的那个大模型，

en: sent me a trademark claim.
zh: 它的母公司却发来商标侵权警告。

en: I had to rename the whole thing while it was taking off.
zh: 我不得不在项目火速起飞的时候 整体改名。

en: You know, they even tried to push me away from the lobster.
zh: 他们甚至连这只龙虾都不放过。

en: (Laughter)
Like, I was staring at the message.
It was like, it's not even the same animal.
zh: 心想：这俩根本就不一样啊！

en: And then they also cut off the model most of my users loved.
zh: 后来，他们连那个大模型也停掉了。

en: You know, first the name,
then the lobster,
then the model.
I was that close to just deleting the whole thing.
zh: 我差点就把整个项目删掉了。

en: But then I learned what people are building with it.
zh: 但后来，我了解到大家是如何使用它的。

en: So at ClawCon in Vienna,
because yes, we have conferences already --
zh: 没错，我们居然已经有大会了。

en: (Laughter)
And people wore lobster headbands,
zh: 大家还戴着龙虾头箍来，

en: I met Stefan
and his 60-year-old dad, Gerhard,
zh: 还有他60岁的父亲格哈德，

en: a beer sommelier who never wrote a single line of code.
zh: 一位从未写过一行代码的啤酒品鉴师。

en: They connected OpenClaw via Bluetooth,
sent it one prompt,
and the agent did the whole 90-minute brew:
zh: 智能体就完成了整整90分钟的酿造过程：

en: temperature ramps, hop additions, everything.
zh: 温度曲线、啤酒花的添加时机等 全部搞定。

en: And then they were like, what are we doing with all this beer?
zh: 然后他们问：这些啤酒怎么办？

en: And the agent was, "Let's make a website."
zh: 智能体说：“要不建个网站？”

en: So they built a website, and then they added payments,
zh: 于是他们建了网站，加了支付功能，

en: and now they have a real product.
zh: 现在他们拥有一个真正的商品。

en: And almost all of it was just done via the phone.
zh: 并且几乎所有操作都是通过手机完成的。

en: In China,
installing OpenClaw is called raising lobsters.
zh: 安装 OpenClaw 被称为“养龙虾”。

en: (Laughter)
Thousands of people were lining up at the Tencent office in Shenzhen
zh: 深圳腾讯大厦外数千人排起了长龙，

en: to get their lobster installed.
Shenzhen even gives out subsidies for people running businesses on OpenClaw.
zh: 深圳甚至为在OpenClaw上 经营业务的人提供补贴。

en: Now
if you install OpenClaw on your work machine,
zh: 如果你在工作电脑上安装OpenClaw，

en: at least with the default settings,
you might get fired.
And then I met an entrepreneur in China
zh: 我还遇到一位中国企业家，

en: who showed me a spreadsheet.
Every employee, every day,
one task automated by OpenClaw.
zh: 必须用OpenClaw自动完成一项任务，

en: If you miss too many days, you're fired.
zh: 漏打卡太多次，就会被开除。

en: So fired for using it,
fired for not using it.
After Marrakesh,
I thought, this is incredible.
zh: 我心想，这东西太厉害了。

en: And it's also a little bit scary.
How can we make it more scary?
(Laughter)
So I added a new feature, a heartbeat.
zh: 于是我加了一个新功能：心跳。

en: You know, by default,
the agent would only wake up when you send it a prompt.
zh: 智能体只有在你发送指令时才会启动。

en: With the heartbeat,
the agent would just wake up periodically,
zh: 智能体会定时自动唤醒，

en: check your emails,
check your calendar, follow up on loose ends.
zh: 查看日历，跟进未完成的事项。

en: My initial prompt was simply "surprise me."
zh: 我给它的初始提示非常简单： “给我个惊喜。”

en: And yes, that's kind of as scary as it sounds.
zh: 听起来确实有那么一点点吓人。

en: So no large company would ship something like that.
zh: 大公司绝对不会发布这样的东西。

en: But I'm a random builder from Austria.
zh: 但我只是个来自奥地利的普通程序猿，

en: I don't have a legal department.
(Laughter)
I built this sandbox, this sandcastle, for me,
zh: 我搭建了这个沙箱环境， 一座给自己玩儿的城堡，

en: and I made it open-source so other people could play with it
zh: 然后开源出来，让更多人也能玩，

en: and other people could raise their imagination.
zh: 让更多人能放飞自己的想象力。

en: Imagine putting an agent into a meeting.
zh: 想象一下把智能体放进会议里，

en: Not for notes, we figured that out.
zh: 不是做会议记录，这已经不稀奇了，

en: A bidirectional model that can listen and hear at the same time.
zh: 而是边听边思考，同步自主执行任务。

en: Somebody mentions a statistic,
a subagent can spin off and check it for you.
zh: 一个子智能体立刻分出去帮你核实。

en: A decision is made,
the agent can send a follow-up
zh: 智能体就能发出跟进通知，

en: before the meeting even ends.
zh: 甚至会议还没散就发完了。

en: In the future,
we're not just going to have one agent.
zh: 我们不会只有一个智能体。

en: You might have your work agent,
your personal Claw, maybe one for health,
maybe one for relationship.
And they all should work together in a secure way.
zh: 它们都应该以安全的方式协同运作。

en: Because how did humanity level up?
By specializing and collaborating.
And agents are about to do the same.
zh: 智能体也即将走上同样的路。

en: Imagine a company, a small business
that has ten agents that are all specialized,
zh: 有十个智能体，各司其职，

en: taking over various parts of the business.
zh: 分别接管不同的业务板块。

en: We don't even have a name for what it might become,
zh: 现在甚至还没有词汇来描述这个场景，

en: but we are about to find out.
So I created the OpenClaw Foundation,
a nonprofit, open-source forever.
zh: 一家非营利机构，永久开源。

en: Because what OpenClaw did for many people
was it moved AI from this scary,
nebulous thing
into something that is fun
and useful and maybe a bit weird.
zh: 有用、可能有点怪的东西。

en: You know, lobsters and headbands and beer businesses.
zh: 从龙虾到龙虾发箍，再到啤酒生意。

en: Because what we need in the future is more people spending more time with AI
zh: 因为我们未来真正需要的， 是让更多人花更多时间与AI相处，

en: to better understand how powerful
and transformative this technology really is.
zh: 究竟有多强大，会带来怎样的变革。

en: In New York,
at ClawCon ...
(Laughter)
Yes, they're everywhere now.
There were thousands of people that were discussing
zh: 成千上万的人聚在一起，

en: what the lobster did this week.
zh: 聊着这周龙虾又干了什么。

en: A retiree in Shenzhen who automated their groceries.
zh: 深圳有位退休人士 用它实现了买菜自动化。

en: A teenager in Sao Paulo
who built a tutoring business on OpenClaw.
Gerhard and his beer machine.
zh: 还有格哈德和他的酿啤酒机器。

en: None of them are programmers.
All of them are builders.
Because that's the real transformation.
zh: 这才是真正的变革所在。

en: It's not a technology.
It's the access.
Agents change who can build things,
zh: 智能体改变的是，谁能创造东西，

en: and that door is not closing again.
Because when you can prompt a prototype into existence
zh: 因为当你只需要一个指令，不到一小时，

en: in one hour,
zh: 就能把原型做出来的时候，

en: anything is possible.
The next breakthrough can come from anyone,
zh: 下一个突破可能来自任何人，

en: any country,
any cafe.
When even a burnt-out founder,
zh: 哪怕是一个精疲力竭的创业者，

en: staring at the screen,
wondering if his spark is gone
zh: 怀疑自己的激情是否已经燃尽，

en: can do something like that.
It's not gone.
It's just waiting.
The lobster is loose,
and it's not going back into the tank.
zh: 它不会再回到水族箱里了。

en: Thank you very much.
(Applause)
Chris Anderson: Come here.
zh: 克里斯·安德森：过来，过来。

en: I mean.
I think I want to say something personal to you.
zh: 我想跟你说几句心里话，

en: With love, but truth.
You really terrify me.
(Laughter)
CA: I’m serious.
If Hollywood was to ever make a movie
zh: 如果好莱坞要拍一部电影，

en: in which humanity opened Pandora’s box,
zh: 讲述人类打开了潘多拉宝盒，

en: and everything went crazy,
like, you seriously could be cast as the star character.
(Laughter)
CA: Because the story we’re told
zh: 克里斯：毕竟我们一直听到的说法是：

en: is that AI researchers are doing all this great stuff,
zh: AI 研究员们一边在做各种了不起的事

en: but they're taking all these great efforts to ensure safety
zh: 一边又在拼命确保安全，

en: and make sure nothing bad could happen.
You take glee in seeing what might happen if you just put it out there.
zh: 可你呢，却偏偏以“直接把它扔向世界， 看看会发生什么”为乐。

en: Like, is any part of you feeling that that's a little bit reckless?
zh: 你内心深处会不会觉得自己这种做法 有点儿……太冒险？

en: Peter Steinberger: I wouldn't say so.
I see my work as a ...
(Laughter)
PS: I see it as a window into the future.
zh: 彼得：我把它看作一扇通往未来的窗。

en: Like, in the very beginning, there were all these scary moments.
zh: 一开始确实出现了各种让人后怕的时刻。

en: Now we have proper security layers.
zh: 但现在我们有了完善的安全机制。

en: You can have your sandbox,
you can put your lobster into a very small, tiny box
zh: 把你的龙虾关进一个非常小的盒子里，

en: and really control what it can do.
There are still some issues that we need to figure out,
zh: 当然还有一些问题需要继续探索。

en: but the fact that so many people want this now
zh: 但正因为现在这么多人都想要这个东西，

en: will help to figure this out much faster.
CA: So I'm glad you mentioned the security layers.
zh: 克里斯：既然你提到了安全机制，

en: I mean, you're making a huge bet on human ingenuity using this.
zh: 我想说，你这么做， 其实是在对人类的智慧进行一场豪赌。

en: This is an incredible tool that suddenly can maximize the power
zh: 这是一个强大得出奇的工具， 能让每个人的能力

en: of what any human can do.
How many people in your community
are taking the safety issue seriously
zh: 有多少人在认真对待安全问题，

en: and want to use OpenClaw, for example, to find smarter ways?
zh: 比如用OpenClaw 来探索更智能的安全方式？

en: Just checking whether anything might be going wrong
zh: 检测是否有什么地方出了问题，

en: and giving an early alarm to someone or something like that?
zh: 然后及时发出预警？或是类似的方式？

en: PS: Most people are not as reckless [as] --
zh: 彼得：大多数人没我这么冒失。

en: number one, putting it into a public Discord --
strongly don't recommend.
Number two, I think I single-handedly increased
Number two, I think I single-handedly increased
zh: 我凭一己之力带火了Mac mini。

en: So most people give it their own little Mac mini.
zh: 大多数人会单独给智能体 配一台Mac mini。

en: Mine's a little princess.
It calls it “The Castle.”
And that greatly reduces the actual risk
zh: 这样风险就大大降低了，

en: because you can only access what's on that computer.
zh: 因为它只能访问那台电脑上的内容，

en: And maybe all your pictures are not there.
zh: 你的私人照片也许不在那上面。

en: CA: Well, definitely, if humanity goes down,
zh: 克里斯：好吧，如果人类就此覆灭，

en: I'll be very grateful for at least the rise in Mac mini sales
zh: 我至少会很感激Mac mini的销量

en: for a period of time.
You are amazing.
You are amazing,
and I think you're actually right at the cutting edge
zh: 我认为你恰恰站在那个最前沿的位置——

en: of whether AI is going to be the biggest boon ever
zh: AI究竟会成为 人类有史以来最大的福音，

en: or possibly a serious problem.
And, you know, I hope you continue conversations with people here
zh: 我希望你能继续在这里与大家交流，

en: and just help us get smarter on how to do this the right way
zh: 并帮助我们更聪明地思考， 到底该如何正确地开启这项事业。

en: because it is absolutely incredible what you've built.
zh: 因为你所做的一切真的令人叹为观止。

en: Thank you for sharing so honestly.
zh: 谢谢你如此坦诚地分享。

en: PS: Thank you so much.
(Applause)
